title: Generate a synthetic respondent CSV from a design file
summary: Reads a MaxDiff (wide) or CBC (long) design CSV, samples a version per fake respondent, and fills task responses under the chosen response format. MaxDiff pick always writes best and worst columns; CBC pick uses one of three modes (standard / none / dual); either design may be written in share (constant-sum) format.
description: The design file is inspected to build a version → task → alternative-labels map. For MaxDiff (wide) the parser auto-detects item columns named 'Item1','Item2',... or purely numeric column headers; supply --design-item-cols to override. For CBC (long) the parser auto-detects an alternative column (alt/alternative/option/concept/profile); if none is found, alternatives are numbered sequentially within each (version, task) pair in row order. Each synthetic respondent is assigned a uniformly random version, then for every task in that version one of the response fillers runs: fill_maxdiff_pick (best + worst, always distinct), fill_cbc_pick (standard/none/dual — 'none' chooses K+1 with probability 0.20, 'dual' adds a _dual column with 50/50 yes/no), or fill_share (integer allocation across K slots summing exactly to share_points; rounding drift corrected on the largest slot). Output columns are: `{respondent_id}`, `{version_col}`, then per-task columns — `{prefix}_{t}_b` + `{prefix}_{t}_w` (MaxDiff pick), `{prefix}_{t}` (+ optional `{prefix}_{t}_dual` for CBC pick), or `{prefix}_{t}_a1`..`{prefix}_{t}_aK` (share). Tasks missing from a respondent's version are written as empty strings.
usage:
[["choice-wrangling::generate-fake-respondents"]]
design_csv = "./data/maxdiff_design.csv"
out_csv = "./data/fake_respondents.csv"
n_respondents = 200
design_type = "maxdiff"
response_format = "pick"
seed = 42

arguments.name: out_csv
arguments.description: Output CSV path. Parent directories are created as needed.

arguments.name: n_respondents
arguments.description: Number of fake respondents to generate.

arguments.name: cbc_mode
arguments.description: CBC pick mode: 'standard' | 'none' | 'dual'. Ignored for MaxDiff and share.

arguments.name: share_points
arguments.description: Total points per task when response_format='share'.

arguments.name: respondent_id
arguments.description: Output column name for the respondent identifier.

arguments.name: version_col
arguments.description: Output column name for the design version. Defaults to 'sys_MXDVersion_{prefix}'.

arguments.name: design_version_col
arguments.description: Design column holding the version. Auto-detected (case-insensitive 'version') when omitted.

arguments.name: design_task_col
arguments.description: Design column holding the task/set. Auto-detected ('set' or 'task') when omitted.

arguments.name: design_item_cols
arguments.description: [MaxDiff] Item columns per task row. Auto-detects 'Item1','Item2',... or numeric column names.

arguments.name: design_alt_col
arguments.description: [CBC] Alternative column. Auto-detects 'alt'/'alternative'/'option'/'concept'/'profile'; otherwise alternatives are numbered by row order.

output_columns.description: Randomly assigned design version for that respondent.
MaxDiff pick: best and worst item IDs for task t (always distinct).
CBC pick: chosen alternative (1..K, or K+1 when cbc_mode='none').
Share format: integer allocation summing exactly to share_points.

notes: design_type and response_format are validated up front; unknown combinations (e.g. maxdiff + dual) fail before generation begins.
MaxDiff pick requires at least 2 items per (version, task); the parser rejects designs where fewer than 2 item columns are non-empty for any task.
CBC parsing requires at least 2 alternatives per (version, task). Alternative labels are preserved as strings (so design label '001' stays '001').
The 'none' mode emits K+1 as the choice value ~20% of the time; downstream decoders must treat K+1 as the no-choice code.
The 'dual' mode always picks a real alternative for {prefix}_{t}; the accompanying _dual column is the yes/no indicator ('2' means the respondent would prefer none even though a pick was forced).
Share allocation uses uniform-random weights + rounding correction so the per-task row sums exactly to share_points even after integer rounding.
Tasks present in the union of all versions but absent from the respondent's assigned version are written as empty strings in the output row.
